# Redis Patterns Applied

> The Redis/Valkey design patterns, taught APPLIED — each shown where the Branded Component System (BCS)
> architecture actually uses it: EchoMQ (the bus) backed by Valkey 9, EchoStore (the near-cache) in front, and
> codemojex (a Telegram emoji-guessing game) the worked consumer. Served at /redis-patterns.

## When & why to read this

Read this when you want a transferable Redis pattern (caching, queues, streams, coordination, time/priority, …) AND
its real application in a shipped system — not a toy. Every pattern is proved with a real key, command, or atomic Lua
script quoted **verbatim** from the as-built echo umbrella (`echo/apps/echo_mq` · `echo_store` · `echo_wire`) and the
codemojex consumer (`echo/apps/codemojex`); nothing is invented, there is no BullMQ, and **Valkey 9 is the only
engine**. Each chapter ends by dooring forward to the dedicated **/echomq** (the protocol in depth) and **/bcs** (the
architecture) courses, and to **/echo-persistence** at the durability frontier. The visual identity is the BCS
"contract-sheet" (light paper, redis-red accent). **Status:** R0–R4 built · R5 opening (landing + four modules; workshop pending)
· R6–R8 specified.

## Navigation

### R0 · Overview — /redis-patterns/overview  ✓ built
Where Valkey sits in codemojex — the EchoMQ bus + the EchoStore near-cache over one Valkey — the reading protocol, the door to R1–R8.
- R0.2 · Valkey under codemojex — /redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game (the facade seam · the two roles · the multi-runtime tier)
- R0.3 · Patterns become protocol — /redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol (the four-layer model · the owned, version-fenced core · the braced `emq:{q}:` data model)

### R1 · Caching — /redis-patterns/caching  ✓ built
The read path: serving reads fast, keeping the cache consistent on writes. → EchoStore · /bcs/cache
- cache-aside · write-through · write-behind · client-side-caching · cache-stampede-prevention · session-management · workshop

### R2 · Coordination & Consistency — /redis-patterns/coordination  ✓ built
Atomicity first — the foundation every later chapter builds on. → /echomq/protocol · /bcs/bus
- atomic-updates · distributed-locking · redlock (contrast) · cross-shard-consistency · hash-tag-colocation · workshop

### R3 · Reliable Queues — /redis-patterns/queues  ✓ built
Wait, active, done, recover — the heart of EchoMQ. → /echomq/queue · /bcs/bus · /echo-persistence
- processing-list · at-least-once · stalled-recovery · atomic-state-machine · blocking-vs-polling · workshop

### R4 · Time, Delay & Priority — /redis-patterns/time-delay-priority  ✓ built
The sorted set as a clock — a timer wheel and a ranking. → /echomq/queue · /bcs/persistence · /echo-persistence
- delayed-queue · schedulers · priority-scores · backoff-retry · leaderboards · workshop

### R5 · Streams & Events — /redis-patterns/streams-events  ◐ opening
The durable, replayable log — a Valkey Stream as the append-only source of truth. Grounded in EchoMQ's shipped Stream Tier (`EchoMQ.Stream` / `StreamConsumer`, keyspace `emq:{q}:stream`). → /echomq/bus · /bcs/bus · /echo-persistence
- streams-event-sourcing ✓ (the-append-only-log · replay-and-rebuild · the-cursor)
- streams-consumer-patterns ✓ (the-blocking-read · consumer-groups · maxlen-trimming)
- pubsub ✓ (fire-and-forget-vs-durable · the-choosing-rule · the-dedicated-blocking-connection)
- custom-events ✓ (domain-events-on-the-stream · windowed-aggregation · reserved-name-discipline)
- workshop  (soon)

### R6 · Flow Control & Scale — /redis-patterns/flow-control  ○ soon
rate-limiting · priorities & fairness · groups & multi-tenant fairness · batches & pipelining · worker-concurrency · workshop

### R7 · Data Modeling & Memory — /redis-patterns/data-modeling  ○ soon
primary-database · memory-optimization · probabilistic (contrast) · bitmaps · vectors & similarity · geospatial · workshop

### R8 · Production & Operations — /redis-patterns/production-operations  ○ soon
kernel-tuning · persistence/pooling/failover · Pinterest · Twitter/X · Uber · operating-EchoMQ · capstone

## The markdown source (deeper docs)

- **Per-page route mirrors** (the source-of-record for every page): `docs/redis-patterns/markdown/<route>.md`
- **The chapter index** (full module ladders + status): `docs/redis-patterns/redis-patterns.toc.md`
- **The grounding map** (which pattern → which real EchoMQ/EchoStore artifact, never invented): `docs/redis-patterns/redis-patterns.roadmap.md`
- **The R ↔ EchoMQ ↔ /bcs door map** (canonical, bidirectional): `docs/redis-patterns/redis-patterns.echomq-doors.md`
- **The reframe contract + per-chapter specs**: `docs/redis-patterns/specs/<slug>/`

## Related courses

- [/echomq](/echomq) — EchoMQ, the protocol in depth (the far side of every "→ EchoMQ" door)
- [/bcs](/bcs) — the Branded Component System these patterns run inside (the figure source)
- [/echo-persistence](/echo-persistence) — the durable floor (where a trimmed / dead-lettered / archived job lands)
- [/elixir](/elixir) — the functional-Elixir craft behind the echo umbrella
